_comment: Canonical cross-language known-answer test (KAT) vectors for Quiver's client-side ciphers, generated once from the Rust reference in crates/quiver-crypto. The Rust, Python, and TypeScript test suites all load and assert THIS file, so any cross-language drift in a cipher fails the build (F-13). Do not edit by hand — regenerate from the Rust reference if a cipher changes, and bump the affected version. See ADR-0031 (DCPE) and ADR-0032 (opaque vectors).

dcpe._note: DCPE v2 (ADR-0035), distance-comparison-preserving. Tolerance-based: the perturbation makes decrypt recover the plaintext only within ~1e-3, while the HMAC tag and the HKDF-derived scale match exactly. Decrypt direction (encrypt uses a fresh random IV; these iv/ciphertext/tag are the reference output for this key at this fixed IV).

opaque_vector._note: Semantically secure client-side opaque vectors (ADR-0032): XChaCha20-Poly1305 over the raw little-endian f32 bytes. Exact interop (not tolerance-based): decrypt recovers the plaintext byte-identically. The envelope is the JSON the server stores and returns verbatim.
